task_completion.description: Verify that at least 3 new partition walls have been created to divide the open shell into rooms.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 2D plan view of Sweet Home 3D, look for at least 3 distinct interior wall segments drawn to create separate rooms or zones.

task_completion.description: Verify that at least 3 doors have been placed in the layout.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for door icons/models intersecting the walls in the 2D plan or visible in the bottom 3D view pane. There should be at least 3 doors.

task_completion.description: Verify that text labels have been added for the functional zones.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for text labels placed in the 2D plan view indicating the different areas (e.g., Reception, Exam Room, Surgery, Kennel, Break Room). There should be labels for at least 5 zones.

task_completion.description: Verify that the clinic is heavily furnished with required items (chairs, tables, shelves, toilet, sink).
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the 2D plan and 3D view for a dense placement of furniture (approximately 35 items total). Key items to spot globally include multiple chairs (at least 6), tables/desks, storage/shelving units (acting as cages/displays), a toilet, and a sink.

task_completion.description: Verify that the project has been saved.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Save' dialog being used and confirmed, or check the application window title bar to ensure the file name 'veterinary_clinic_starter.sh3d' does not have an asterisk (*) next to it, indicating there are no unsaved changes.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Sweet Home 3D graphical interface to design the clinic.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent clicking tools, dragging furniture from the catalog, and drawing walls in the Sweet Home 3D GUI, rather than running terminal commands to modify the .sh3d file directly.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the genuine Sweet Home 3D application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The standard Sweet Home 3D interface (catalog on left, 2D plan top right, 3D view bottom right, furniture list bottom left) must be visible.

integrity.description: Verify the agent built upon the provided starter file rather than opening a pre-completed file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The window title should show 'veterinary_clinic_starter.sh3d' and early screenshots should show an empty building shell before furniture and interior walls are added.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires 100 total points with a passing threshold of 70. The scoring breakdown is: 20 points for at least 3 partition walls, 20 points for at least 3 doors, 15 points for text labels of the zones, 25 points for furniture placement, and 20 points for saving the project. Note: The underlying verification script uses global counts for furniture rather than strict zone-specific counts. If the total number of required items (e.g., 6 chairs, 3 tables, a toilet, a sink) is present anywhere in the layout, it satisfies the furniture requirement. You do not need to count exactly 35 items if the space appears comprehensively furnished with the key required elements.
